body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule G 2025 capital gains and losses.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.G-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule G numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 1000
Value: 50
Value: 0.50
Value: 100
Value: 1.00
Value: 10000000

Schedule G Part B calculates capital property gains and losses not covered by parts C, D or E. Line 10 subtracts adjusted cost base and expenses from proceeds for shares and mutual fund units. Line 12 does the same for bonds, debt securities, partnership interests and other securities or property. Line 14 does the same for immovables and depreciable property, with the form instruction not to take into account losses on depreciable property. Line 16 covers personal-use property. Line 18 covers precious property, line 19 enters previous-year losses on precious property, and line 20 subtracts line 19 from line 18 and floors the result at zero. Page 5 says gains on precious or personal-use property do not have to be reported if proceeds are $1,000 or less; otherwise the adjusted cost base is $1,000 or the actual adjusted cost base, whichever is greater. Line 26 adds lines 10, 12, 14, 16, 20, 21, 22 and 24. Line 30 subtracts line 28 from line 26. Line 34 subtracts line 32 from line 30. Line 40 subtracts line 38 from line 36. Line 44 adds lines 34 and 40 and carries the result to line 88.

Schedule G Part C calculates resource property. Line 46 subtracts adjusted cost base and expenses from proceeds. Line 50 adds lines 46 through 48 and carries the result to line 88. Part D calculates qualified farm or fishing property and qualified small business corporation shares. Line 54 subtracts adjusted cost base and expenses from proceeds. Line 60 adds lines 54, 56 and 58. Line 68 subtracts line 63 from line 60. Line 80 subtracts line 77 from line 75. Line 84 adds lines 68 and 80 and carries the result to line 88.

Schedule G Part E calculates shares disposed of as part of a qualifying business transfer or qualifying cooperative conversion. Line 203 subtracts adjusted cost base and expenses from proceeds. Line 206 adds lines 203 through 205. Line 207.1 subtracts line 207 from line 206. Line 207.4 subtracts line 207.3 from line 207.2. Line 208 adds lines 207.1 and 207.4 and carries the result to line 88. Page 5 says the maximum capital gains deduction for a qualifying business transfer or qualifying cooperative conversion is $10 million.

Schedule G Part F calculates taxable capital gains or net capital loss. Line 88 adds lines 44, 50, 84 and 208, whether positive or negative. Line 94 enters the deferral of capital gains realized on the disposition of small business corporation shares. Line 94.1 subtracts line 94 from line 88. Line 107 applies the 50% inclusion rate, and line 107.1 multiplies line 94.1 by 50%. Line 107.2 enters capital gains to which the 100% rate applies further to the disposition of an interest in a partnership. Line 108 adds lines 107.1 and 107.2. If line 108 is positive, it is carried to TP-1 line 139; if negative, the schedule treats it as a net capital loss.
